nodes.name: TikTok – Init Video Upload
nodes.notes:
TikTok Content Posting API – Direct Publish with PULL_FROM_URL.

⚠️  IMPORTANT SETUP:
1. Create an OAuth2 credential in n8n with:
   • Client ID / Secret from TikTok Developer Portal
   • Authorization URL: https://www.tiktok.com/v2/auth/authorize/
   • Access Token URL: https://open.tiktokapis.com/v2/oauth/token/
   • Scope: video.publish
2. TikTok does NOT natively support schedule_time via this endpoint.
   Use n8n's built-in Wait / Cron node to delay execution.
3. Replace $json.video_url with the public URL of your .mp4 (9:16 vertical, ≤4 GB, ≤10 min).
4. Replace $json.caption with your desired caption text.
5. privacy_level options: SELF_ONLY | MUTUAL_FOLLOW_FRIENDS | FOLLOWER_OF_CREATOR | PUBLIC_TO_EVERYONE

nodes.name: IG Reels – Step 1: Create Media Container
nodes.notes:
Meta Graph API – IG Reels Container Creation (Step 1 of 2).

⚠️  IMPORTANT SETUP:
1. Create an OAuth2 credential in n8n with:
   • Client ID / Secret from Meta Developer Portal
   • Authorization URL: https://www.facebook.com/v21.0/dialog/oauth
   • Access Token URL: https://graph.facebook.com/v21.0/oauth/access_token
   • Scope: instagram_basic,instagram_content_publish,pages_read_engagement
2. $json.ig_user_id = your Instagram Business/Creator Account ID (numeric).
3. $json.video_url = public HTTPS URL to a vertical .mp4 (9:16, ≤1 GB).
4. $json.captions = array of caption lines, e.g. ["Line 1", "Line 2"].
   Falls back to $json.caption (single string) if array is absent.
5. $json.schedule_epoch = Unix epoch timestamp (10+ min in future, ≤75 days).
6. scheduled_publish_time only works when the IG account is a Business account.

nodes.name: IG Reels – Step 2: Publish Container
nodes.notes:
Meta Graph API – IG Reels Publish (Step 2 of 2).

This node publishes the container created in Step 1.
• $json.body.id references the container ID from the previous node.
• If you used scheduled_publish_time in Step 1, Meta will auto-publish
  at the scheduled time—you still need to call this publish endpoint
  to finalize the container.

nodes.name: YT Shorts – Step 1: Init Resumable Upload
nodes.notes:
YouTube Data API v3 – Resumable Upload Init (Step 1 of 2).

⚠️  IMPORTANT SETUP:
1. Create an OAuth2 credential in n8n with:
   • Client ID / Secret from Google Cloud Console
   • Authorization URL: https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth
   • Access Token URL: https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token
   • Scope: https://www.googleapis.com/auth/youtube.upload https://www.googleapis.com/auth/youtube
2. Enable "YouTube Data API v3" in your Google Cloud project.
3. This node returns HTTP 200 with a 'Location' header containing
   the resumable upload URI. Pass that URI to Step 2.
4. SCHEDULING: YouTube uses 'publishAt' (ISO 8601) + privacyStatus='private'.
   The node converts your epoch timestamp to ISO automatically.
   $json.schedule_epoch = Unix epoch (seconds).
   $json.schedule_iso  = ISO 8601 string (takes priority if provided).
5. categoryId '20' = Gaming. Change as needed:
   https://developers.google.com/youtube/v3/docs/videoCategories/list
6. containsSyntheticMedia = true because content is AI-generated.
   Required since Oct 2024 update.
7. #Shorts in title OR description signals YouTube to classify as a Short.
8. Video must be ≤60s and 9:16 vertical for guaranteed Shorts treatment.
9. Quota cost: ~1600 units per upload (dropping to ~100 after Dec 2025).
   Default daily quota: 10,000 units.

nodes.name: YT Shorts – Step 2: Upload Video Binary
nodes.notes:
YouTube Data API v3 – Binary Upload (Step 2 of 2).

This node sends the actual .mp4 file bytes to the resumable upload URI
returned by Step 1 (in the 'Location' response header).

• $json.headers.location = the resumable upload URI from Step 1.
• The binary field 'data' must contain your .mp4 file.
  In your upstream flow, use a 'Read Binary File' node or
  'Google Drive Download' node to load the video into the
  'data' binary property before this node executes.
• On success, YouTube returns the full video resource JSON
  including the videoId. The video will auto-publish at the
  'publishAt' time you set in Step 1.
• Timeout is set to 5 minutes to accommodate large uploads.

nodes.name: FB Reels – Publish Video
nodes.notes:
Meta Graph API – Facebook Reels Publish.

⚠️  IMPORTANT SETUP:
1. Uses the SAME Meta OAuth2 credential as IG Reels.
   Additional scope needed: pages_manage_posts,pages_read_engagement
2. $json.fb_page_id = your Facebook Page ID (numeric).
   FB Reels are published to Pages, not personal profiles.
3. $json.video_url = public HTTPS URL to a vertical .mp4 (9:16).
4. video_state = 'SCHEDULED' enables scheduled publishing.
   Omit or set to 'PUBLISHED' for immediate posting.
5. $json.schedule_epoch = Unix epoch timestamp for future publish.
6. $json.captions = array of caption lines, joined with spaces.
   Falls back to $json.caption (single string).

main.node: YT Shorts – Step 2: Upload Video Binary
